title: ophrys lutea subsp. phryganae
intro: Ophrys lutea subsp. phryganae, also known as the yellow bee orchid, is a subspecies of orchid that belongs to the genus Ophrys, a group of flowering plants known for their unique and often insect-like appearance. The yellow bee orchid has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are said to be beneficial for treating various health conditions. The medicinal properties of Ophrys lutea subsp. phryganae are believed to provide relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and antiseptic properties. Herbal preparations of the yellow bee orchid, including teas and infusions, are used to treat these conditions, as well as to soothe digestive issues and promote overall well-being.
